questions.question: Explain what light is and why it is important for vision.
questions.answer: Light is a form of energy that enables us to see objects. When light reflects from objects and enters our eyes, we are able to see them.

questions.question: Describe the reflection of light with an example.
questions.answer: Reflection of light is the bouncing back of light when it strikes a surface. For example, when light hits a mirror it reflects and forms an image.

questions.question: State and explain the laws of reflection.
questions.answer: The laws of reflection state that the angle of incidence is equal to the angle of reflection and the incident ray, reflected ray and normal lie in the same plane.

questions.question: Explain the characteristics of images formed by a plane mirror.
questions.answer: Images formed by a plane mirror are virtual, upright, of the same size as the object and laterally inverted.

questions.question: Explain the term lateral inversion with an example.
questions.answer: Lateral inversion is the sideways reversal of an image in a mirror. For example, the left hand appears as the right hand in the mirror.

questions.question: Differentiate between concave mirrors and convex mirrors.
questions.answer: A concave mirror curves inward and can focus light rays, while a convex mirror curves outward and spreads light rays.

questions.question: Explain two uses of concave mirrors.
questions.answer: Concave mirrors are used as shaving mirrors and in vehicle headlights because they can focus light.

questions.question: Explain why convex mirrors are used as rear-view mirrors in vehicles.
questions.answer: Convex mirrors provide a wider field of view and allow drivers to see more area behind the vehicle.

questions.question: Explain the difference between reflection and refraction of light.
questions.answer: Reflection is the bouncing back of light from a surface, while refraction is the bending of light when it passes from one medium to another.

questions.question: Describe how a convex lens forms images.
questions.answer: A convex lens converges light rays and can form real or virtual images depending on the position of the object.

questions.question: Explain the working of a magnifying glass.
questions.answer: A magnifying glass uses a convex lens to produce a larger image of a nearby object.

questions.question: Explain how concave lenses affect light rays.
questions.answer: Concave lenses diverge light rays and form virtual, upright and smaller images.

questions.question: Describe two uses of convex lenses.
questions.answer: Convex lenses are used in magnifying glasses and cameras to focus light.

questions.question: Describe two uses of concave lenses.
questions.answer: Concave lenses are used in spectacles to correct short-sightedness and in optical instruments.

questions.question: Explain how mirrors are useful in everyday life.
questions.answer: Mirrors are used in vehicles, bathrooms, optical instruments and solar devices to reflect light.

questions.question: Explain how lenses are used in cameras.
questions.answer: Camera lenses focus light from objects onto the film or sensor to produce clear images.

questions.question: Explain how telescopes use lenses to observe distant objects.
questions.answer: Telescopes use lenses to collect and focus light from distant objects to produce enlarged images.

questions.question: Explain why light travels in straight lines.
questions.answer: Light travels in straight lines in a uniform medium because it moves without bending unless it encounters a different medium or obstacle.

questions.question: Explain how shadows are formed.
questions.answer: Shadows are formed when an opaque object blocks the path of light.

questions.question: Explain how periscopes use mirrors.
questions.answer: Periscopes use two plane mirrors placed at angles to reflect light and allow viewing objects from hidden positions.

questions.question: Explain how microscopes use lenses.
questions.answer: Microscopes use convex lenses to magnify very small objects so they can be seen clearly.

questions.question: Explain the importance of studying light in science.
questions.answer: Studying light helps us understand vision, develop optical instruments and improve technologies like cameras and telescopes.

questions.question: Explain how mirrors and lenses help improve modern technology.
questions.answer: Mirrors and lenses are used in cameras, microscopes, telescopes and medical equipment to control and focus light.

questions.question: Explain how optical devices use reflection and refraction.
questions.answer: Optical devices use reflection from mirrors and refraction through lenses to control and direct light.

questions.question: Explain how light, mirrors and lenses are interconnected in optical science.
questions.answer: Light interacts with mirrors through reflection and with lenses through refraction, allowing images to be formed and optical instruments to function.
